content:
Hey everyone!
I picked up this issue: https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/issues/975, It was marked as "good first issue" so I thought I might give it a try.

From what I understood, zerocopy::IntoBytes is a drop in replacement for transmute::AsBytes with some minor differences. So I grep'ed for AsBytes and found it being used in three files, in debugfs/traits.rs, dma.rs and uaccess.rs. I have replaced most of them but some requires additional changes

  1. for BinaryWriter it requires that T is marked with zerocopy::Immutable, is that safe to do without changing the interface?

  2. for BInaryReaderMut it requires that T is marked with zerocopy::FromBytes, is it okay to replace transmute::FromBytes to zerocopy::FromBytes for this one instance because it is a separate issue on its own, see: https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/issues/1241

I am extremely new to rust for the kernel and to linux in general, so please let me know if I have misunderstood anything

thank you!
